svg.text: Mr. Mulkey’s 5:00 Second grade kidstuff class handbook 2017-2018
Introduction
Dear Second Grade Parent,
My name is Jonathan Mulkey, and I am so excited to be your child’s Kidstuff teacher at 5:00 this
year!
I have been with North Hill’s children’s ministry since 2006 and have taught second graders
every year.
My class is highly structured around a U.S. Army theme.
I borrowed and adapted this theme from
my dad, a retired Army major and Sunday school teacher.
Students can earn promotions through
the ranks as they accumulate points.
They earn points by completing two weekly “missions”:
Mission 1: Complete Activity Sheet (3 points)
Each Sunday, a two-sided activity sheet is sent home with students.
Students complete this sheet
during the week and turn it in the next Sunday.
The sheet must be completely and neatly done
for full credit (students will be given the opportunity to redo or complete if this is not the case).
Mission 2: Recite Memory Verse (3 points)
Stapled to each activity sheet is a memory verse.
Students memorize this verse during the week
and recite it the next Sunday. A few verses throughout the year may be worth more than 3 points
based on length/difficulty.
The verse must be said completely and correctly with no more than a
one-word prompt allowed for full credit (students will be given the opportunity to recite again if
this is not the case).
Absences
If your child is absent from class, they can still complete their missions and you can send the
completed missions to me by email or phone at jhmulkey@gmail.com or (864) 569-4879.
Simply
scan or take a photo of each page of their completed activity sheet and record an audio or video clip
of them reciting their memory verse.
All missions are made available on the class website:
jhmulkey.wixsite.com/ksgrade2
Late Missions
In order to be fair to students who are diligently completing their missions each week, 1 point will be
subtracted from any missions completed more than three weeks after the assigned date, and an
additional 1 point will be subtracted from any missions completed more than six weeks after the
assigned date.
So, it is not worth it for students to procrastinate and then complete a bunch of
missions at once, as they will only be able to earn a fraction of the points.
Slow and steady wins the
race!
Bonus Missions
At least once each quarter, I will assign a “bonus mission” that students can complete for extra
points.
Weekly Prize Drawing
In addition to prizes earned with promotions, each week I will draw one name at random, and if that
student is in class, they will get to choose a prize from the prize drawer at the end of class.
Any
single student may only win the drawing once each quarter.
Just another reason to attend class
J
